sections.instruction: Questions 1-4: Complete the form below. Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS AND/ OR A NUMBER for each answer. Questions 5-7: Choose THREE letters A-G. Questions 8-10: Complete the form below. Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS AND/ OR A NUMBER for each answer.

questions.content:
Model – Ford Fiesta
Registration: 3R1 {{BLANK}}
Current mileage: {{BLANK}} miles

Estimated yearly mileage: 6,000 a year
Overnight parking: {{BLANK}}

Cover: third party {{BLANK}}
No claim bonus: 6 years
Which additional cover does Liam want?

sections.instruction: Questions 11-14: Complete the sentences below. Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS AND/ OR A NUMBER for each answer. Questions 15-17: Choose the correct letter A, B or C. Questions 18-20: Choose THREE letters A-F.

questions.content: Every year more than {{BLANK}} people try to climb Mont Blanc.
Approximately {{BLANK}} reach the top every day in the summer months.
In July 2007 there were 30 fatalities, chiefly due to {{BLANK}}
One danger is {{BLANK}} caused by other climbers.
How did Katherine do her most recent climb?
Why did Katherine find her first climb on Mont Blanc difficult?
options: A because of poor weather conditions
B because of the time she was climbing
C because the route was crowded

questions.content: What did Katherine do on her second climb that made it easier?
options: A she spent a few days in Charnonix beforehand
B she did the climb over two days
C she climbed smaller peaks for training

questions.content: What has experience taught Katherine about climbing Mont Blanc?
options: A be in good physical condition
B have the right climbing equipment
C wear several layers of clothing
D protect yourself from the sun
F acclimatize yourself to the altitude first

sections.instruction: Questions 21-23: Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS for each answer. Questions 24-28: Complete the sentences below. Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS for each answer. Questions 29 and 30: Choose the correct letter A, B or C.

questions.content: When do Elaine and Adam decide to meet for revision? {{BLANK}}
What item does Adam think will help them work independently on revision? {{BLANK}}
How does Elaine suggest they spend their revision time between meetings? {{BLANK}}
It’s best to avoid notes {{BLANK}}
Relate {{BLANK}} to key theories and arguments.
Using diagrams can help keep a lot of information {{BLANK}}
Bullet points can enable you to highlight {{BLANK}}
Keep a record of your sources, such as the {{BLANK}}
In future revision sessions Adam suggests using past papers
options: A to try to predict future exam questions

questions.content: Elaine suggests
options: A adding essay practice to the timetable
B doing essays under exam conditions
C asking a tutor to mark their essays

sections.instruction: Questions 31-33: Choose the correct letter A, B or C. Questions 34-37: Match the groups A-D with their concerns about shared space 34-37. Questions 38 and 39: Choose TWO letters A-D. Question 40: Choose the correct letter A, B or C.

questions.content: According to the text, shared space
options: A is very popular in the Netherlands
B reduces the number of cars in an area
C improves the dynamic between drivers and pedestrians

questions.content: Shared space design results in
options: B stronger boundaries between pedestrians and motorists

questions.content: Supporters of shared space argue that it
options: A leads to new businesses opening
B makes people happier to shop in the area

questions.content: Match the groups A-D with their concerns about shared space.
Insufficient experience amongst planners {{BLANK}}
A lack of important signage {{BLANK}}
Intimidation by motorists {{BLANK}}
The loss of familiar street furniture {{BLANK}}
People will be able to answer the questions
Questions must be designed so that they
options: A do not reflect the views of the campaign group
B do not require an understanding of shared space
